success_surface: The agent successfully solved a multi-condition cell-level marking task by combining comprehensive data exploration, hybrid library usage (pandas for logic + openpyxl for formatting), and systematic validation.
effective_behaviors: Conducted full structural analysis (sheet names, dimensions, columns, value distributions) before implementing any logic
Used pandas for complex grouping/aggregation logic while maintaining openpyxl for file I/O to preserve formatting
Validated intermediate results through print statements before writing final output
Tracked sequential state changes (e.g., detecting when a week number changed from previous row) for conditional marking
Created deep copies of workbooks prior to modification to prevent unintended mutations
Grouped data by multiple keys (Employee + Week) to evaluate compound conditions
Logged execution statistics (rows affected, values written, ranges modified) for verification
evidence: Agent printed sheet names, dimensions, columns, unique values, and value counts before writing any solution code
Loaded workbook with openpyxl for formatting preservation, then read data with pandas for processing
Printed valid combinations and sample results before committing to final write operation
Maintained prev_emp and prev_week variables to detect new week boundaries in sequential iteration
Created wb = load_workbook() and wrote directly to ws without modifying source file
Counted and reported total 'y' values written and exact cell range F2:F92 in final log

memory_items.title: Exploration Before Implementation
memory_items.lesson: Always examine complete spreadsheet structure before coding solutions
memory_items.generalization: Inspect sheet names, dimensions, column headers, unique values, and data distributions programmatically to understand the problem space fully before writing transformation logic.

memory_items.title: Hybrid Library Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Combine pandas for data logic with openpyxl for formatting preservation
memory_items.generalization: Use pandas for heavy data manipulation, filtering, and aggregation; use openpyxl for loading/saving files to retain original styles, formulas, merged cells, and layout elements.

memory_items.title: Sequential State Detection
memory_items.lesson: Track previous row state to identify transition points in ordered data
memory_items.generalization: When marking based on boundary conditions (like week changes, category shifts), maintain state variables from the previous row to detect when transitions occur.

memory_items.title: Compound Condition Grouping
memory_items.lesson: Group by multiple keys to evaluate multi-criteria conditions efficiently
memory_items.generalization: For tasks requiring presence/absence checks across categories within groups, use groupby() with multiple key columns to aggregate and validate conditions per group.

memory_items.title: Validation-Driven Development
memory_items.lesson: Print intermediate results to verify logic before finalizing
memory_items.generalization: Output sample computations, qualifying records, and expected counts during development to catch logical errors before writing permanent changes.

memory_items.title: Formatting Preservation Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Load workbooks for editing rather than replacing them entirely
memory_items.generalization: Create a copy or load existing workbook with formatting-aware libraries, modify only target cells in place, then save—avoid operations that strip styles or metadata.

memory_items.title: Execution Audit Trail
memory_items.lesson: Log modification statistics for verification and debugging
memory_items.generalization: After completion, report rows/cells affected, values written, and any skipped entries to enable quick validation of scope and accuracy.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Exploration Before Implementation**: Examine complete spreadsheet structure programmatically before writing solutions—including sheet names, dimensions, column headers, unique values, and data distributions.
- **Hybrid Library Strategy**: Combine pandas for data logic with openpyxl for formatting preservation; use pandas for heavy manipulation/filtering/aggregation, openpyxl for I/O to retain styles/formulas/merged cells.
- **Sequential State Detection**: Track previous row state (values, identifiers) to detect transition points like new weeks, category shifts, or block boundaries for conditional marking.
- **Compound Condition Grouping**: Use groupby() with multiple key columns to evaluate presence/absence criteria within each group for efficient multi-criteria validation.
- **Validation-Driven Development**: Output sample computations, qualifying records, and expected counts during development to catch logical errors before writing permanent changes.
- **Formatting Preservation Protocol**: Load existing workbook with formatting-aware libraries, modify only target cells in place, then save—avoid full file replacement operations.
- **Execution Audit Trail**: After completion, report rows/cells affected, values written, and any skipped entries to enable quick validation of scope and accuracy.
